definition: Priority rules for resolving contradictory signals between ICT concepts. Higher-layer concepts override lower-layer ones. Essential for the bridge scoring engine to handle ambiguous setups.
feeds_into: cross_correlations (synergies are the positive counterpart to conflicts)
bridge_integration: Partially integrated, partially informational. The file IS loaded — bridge/claude_decision.py:291 calls _load_json('ict_concepts/conflict_resolution.json') and reads .get('rules', []) into a local variable inside _build_layer_narrative. However the 14-rule array is currently NOT iterated programmatically — the 'CONFLICTS DETECTED' section in the prompt (claude_decision.py:303-306) is populated from two HARDCODED checks at lines 295-301 ('Displacement in accumulation phase' when has_displacement + Asian session; 'OB without displacement = unproven zone' when ob_score >= 10 and not has_displacement). The remaining rules from this card are surfaced INDIRECTLY via dedicated code paths, not via this loader: (1) HTF-overrides-LTF — claude_decision.py HTF Alignment Gate (pre-gate #5, ARCHITECTURE.md § 4.1) hard-blocks non-scalp counter-H4 entries; the HTF Data Gate (#4) caps Grade A→B if W1/D1/H4 missing. (2) FVG-in-wrong-PD-zone — synergy_scorer.py:524-530 'FVG in wrong premium/discount zone' gate (-3); the parent ZONE GATE at claude_decision.py:575 hard-SKIPs all wrong-zone entries. (3) OB-without-displacement — ict_pipeline.py:509-512 detect_order_blocks(require_fvg=True) at detection time; synergy_scorer.py:482-486 'Displacement gates OB validity' (-4); claude_decision.py:299-301 conflict line. (4) Kill-zone-without-sweep — handled by claude_decision.py:614-648 KILL ZONE GATE (Grade A + displacement_confirmed override required to trade outside) plus the DOL pre-filter (line 657-684); not the same as the card's rule but the closest-fit enforcement. (5) SMT-without-CHoCH — currently NOT enforced; SMT contributes to liquidity_score (scorer.py) only as confluence, not as a blocker. (6) OTE-without-FVG/OB — synergy_scorer.py:476-480 'Premium/Discount gates OTE' (-5) and ict_pipeline.py:880-911 entry-priority sort (FVG+OB > FVG-in-OTE > plain FVG) prefer the zone-based entry over pure OTE. (7) PO3-accumulation-vs-clear-HTF-structure — claude_decision.py:295-297 conflict line + hard KILL ZONE GATE for non-crypto Asian-session entries. (8) CISD-without-CHoCH — synergy_scorer.py 'CISD + PO3 phase transition' (+5) requires distribution phase; no hard 'CISD without CHoCH = reduce size' enforcement, but the synergy is conditional on phase. (9) Order pairing TP vs Fibonacci TP — handled by claude_decision.py prompt instruction (line 821-823: 'TP at next liquidity target' for TP1, '1.272/1.618 fib if available'); no explicit priority resolver between the two — it's left to Claude's judgment. (10) Quarterly shift vs daily bias — synergy_scorer.py:163-183 _has_quarterly_shift / _quarterly_aligned / _quarterly_opposed feed 'Quarterly shift alignment' (+5) and 'counter_quarterly_shift' gate (-6); the swing-vs-intraday split from the card is not codified. (11) BPR blocks path to TP — NOT integrated; balanced_price_range card exists but no detector in the bridge populates a BPR-obstacle factor. (12) Volume-profile-validates-FVG / OB-at-LVN — partially: synergy_scorer.py:264-280 _ob_at_high_volume uses FVG_stack / HiddenOB as HVN proxy ('OB at high-volume zone' synergy +3); LVN penalty is not implemented. (13) London-delayed vs kill zone active — NOT integrated; intraday_profile concept exists but no detector. The 'rules' list in this JSON should therefore be treated as: (a) reference documentation for Claude's reasoning when she reads the KB directly, and (b) a target list for future codification — not as a runtime rules engine. Rules (5), (11), (13) and the BPR aspect of (12) are real gaps if a future session wants to fill them.
_purpose: When concepts give contradictory signals, use these rules to resolve. Higher-layer concepts OVERRIDE lower-layer ones.

rules.conflict: LTF structure says SELL but HTF structure says BUY
rules.resolution: HTF WINS. The LTF SELL signal is a pullback within the HTF trend, not a reversal. Look for LTF BUY entries inside the pullback.

rules.conflict: FVG says enter but price is in wrong premium/discount zone
rules.resolution: premium_discount WINS. The FVG may be valid technically but entering in the wrong zone means poor R:R. Reduce grade by 1 or skip.

rules.conflict: OB is present but no displacement created it
rules.resolution: displacement requirement WINS. An OB without displacement is just a candle — no institutional proof. Skip the OB.

rules.conflict: Kill zone is active but no liquidity sweep occurred yet
rules.resolution: Wait. Kill zone provides the WINDOW but liquidity_sweep provides the TRIGGER. Being in a kill zone without a sweep = no entry yet.

rules.conflict: SMT says reversal but structure hasn't changed (no CHoCH)
rules.resolution: Wait for structure confirmation. SMT is a LEADING indicator, structure is CONFIRMING. SMT without CHoCH = early warning, not entry signal.

rules.conflict: OTE zone doesn't overlap with FVG/OB
rules.resolution: Prefer FVG/OB as entry over pure Fibonacci level. OTE is precision refinement WITHIN entry zones, not a standalone entry method.

rules.conflict: PO3 says accumulation (don't trade) but HTF structure is very clear
rules.resolution: PO3 timing WINS for intraday. Clear HTF structure is for swing trades with wider stops. For intraday, wait for manipulation + distribution phases.

rules.conflict: CISD fires but no CHoCH confirmed yet
rules.resolution: CISD is early warning — enter with reduced size. Full size only after CHoCH confirms. CISD anticipates CHoCH but doesn't guarantee it.

rules.conflict: Volume profile shows HVN at entry but FVG says enter
rules.resolution: Volume profile VALIDATES the FVG. An FVG at an HVN is stronger — institutional defense confirmed. Enter with higher conviction.

rules.conflict: OB at LVN (low volume node) — institutional zone but no volume
rules.resolution: Reduce conviction. OB at LVN means the zone was NOT heavily defended. Price may accelerate through without reacting.

rules.conflict: Order pairing says TP at BSL but fibonacci extension says TP at 1.618
rules.resolution: Order pairing (liquidity-based TP) takes priority over Fibonacci (mathematical TP). If BSL is before 1.618 extension → TP at BSL. If BSL is beyond 1.618 → use 1.618 as TP1, BSL as TP2.

rules.conflict: Intraday profile says London Delayed but kill zone is active
rules.resolution: Wait. London Delayed means manipulation is pushed to NY. Don't force entries during London just because the kill zone window is open.

rules.conflict: Quarterly shift conflicts with daily bias
rules.resolution: Quarterly shift WINS for swing trades. Daily bias WINS for intraday. A new quarterly shift takes days to fully express — intraday can still move counter to it.

rules.conflict: Balanced price range blocks the path to TP
rules.resolution: BPR = strong obstacle. If a BPR sits between entry and TP, price may stall. Either set TP at the BPR (closer target) or wait for BPR to be broken before entering.
